data.title: chore: remove remaining skogix-local paths from settings.json
data.description:
## Problem

Two `/home/skogix/...` paths remain in `.claude/settings.json` after the reorg. Neither breaks this container (one is ignored, the other fails gracefully on load), but both should be cleaned up.

## Remaining paths

### 1\. `autoMemoryDirectory` (line \~152)

```json
"autoMemoryDirectory": "/home/skogix/claude/.skogai/memory"
```

* Points to the old home path
* Per the Claude Code docs (`.skogai/docs/claude-code/memory.md:335`): **"This setting is accepted from policy, local, and user settings. It is not accepted from project settings (**`.claude/settings.json`) to prevent a shared project from redirecting auto memory writes to sensitive locations."
* So it is silently ignored — has no effect where it is
* Should either be **removed** from `.claude/settings.json` or **moved** to `.claude/settings.local.json` / user settings, with a portable path (tilde-expanded supported, e.g. `~/.skogai/memory`)

### 2\. Worktrunk marketplace (line \~137)

```json
"worktrunk": {
  "source": { "source": "directory", "path": "/home/skogix/.local/src/worktrunk" }
}
```

* Only exists on skogix's workstation
* On any other container this marketplace just fails to resolve (non-fatal but noisy)
* Options:
  * Move to `settings.local.json` (user-specific override)
  * Publish worktrunk as a git-source marketplace and swap the config to that
  * Keep as-is and document that it's skogix-only

## Already fixed in branch `claude/review-home-folder-changes-tVavu`

* `CLAUDE_CODE_TMPDIR` removed (was hardcoded to `/home/skogix/claude/tmp`, caused every Bash call to fail its cwd-tracking write) — commit `b118503`
* `additionalDirectories` array removed — commit `b118503`
* Hook paths for `claude-md-linecheck` and `healthcheck` switched to `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` — commit `45e360a`

## Related

Not a duplicate of [#25](https://linear.app/skogai/issue/SKO-160/feat-document-all-binariesscripts-into-claudemd), which covers documentation rather than portability.
